title: Convolvulus crinicalyx
intro: Convolvulus crinicalyx, also known as the 'creeping morning glory', is a perennial vine native to the tropical regions of India and Africa, characterized by its twining stems and small, white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Convolvulus crinicalyx include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Convolvulus crinicalyx is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and its extracts are also used in modern herbal remedies for their potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Convolvulus crinicalyx belongs to the family Convolvulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bindweeds or morning glories. The genus Convolvulus is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 200 species distributed across the world. Convolvulus crinicalyx is native to the tropical regions of Africa, where it grows as a perennial or annual herb. This plant is classified as a type of vine or climber, with long, twining stems that support its climbing habit. It is also known by the common names "thorny bindweed" or "thorny morning glory".
